title: Institute of Mathematical Statistics

infobox.value: (IMS) is an international professional and [scholarly society](/wiki/Learned_society "Learned society") devoted to the development, dissemination, and application of [statistics](/wiki/Statistics "Statistics") and [probability](/wiki/Probability "Probability"). The Institute currently has about 4,000 members in all parts of the world. Beginning in 2005, the institute started offering joint membership with the [Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability](/wiki/Bernoulli_Society_for_Mathematical_Statistics_and_Probability "Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability") as well as with the [International Statistical Institute](/wiki/International_Statistical_Institute "International Statistical Institute"). The Institute was founded in 1935 with [Harry C. Carver](/wiki/Harry_C._Carver "Harry C. Carver") and [Henry L. Rietz](/wiki/Henry_Louis_Rietz "Henry Louis Rietz") as its two most important supporters. The institute publishes a variety of journals, and holds several international conference every year.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

International learned society for mathematical statistics

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This article **may rely excessively on sources [too closely associated with the subject](/wiki/Wikipedia:NIS "Wikipedia:NIS")**, potentially preventing the article from being [verifiable](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability") and [neutral](/wiki/Wikipedia:Neutral_point_of_view "Wikipedia:Neutral point of view"). Please help [improve it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Institute_of_Mathematical_Statistics&action=edit) by replacing them with more appropriate [citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") to [reliable, independent sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Independent_sources "Wikipedia:Independent sources"). *(May 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The **Institute of Mathematical Statistics** (IMS) is an international professional and [scholarly society](/wiki/Learned_society "Learned society") devoted to the development, dissemination, and application of [statistics](/wiki/Statistics "Statistics") and [probability](/wiki/Probability "Probability"). The Institute currently has about 4,000 members in all parts of the world. Beginning in 2005, the institute started offering joint membership with the [Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability](/wiki/Bernoulli_Society_for_Mathematical_Statistics_and_Probability "Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability") as well as with the [International Statistical Institute](/wiki/International_Statistical_Institute "International Statistical Institute"). The Institute was founded in 1935 with [Harry C. Carver](/wiki/Harry_C._Carver "Harry C. Carver") and [Henry L. Rietz](/wiki/Henry_Louis_Rietz "Henry Louis Rietz") as its two most important supporters. The institute publishes a variety of journals, and holds several international conference every year.

## Publications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Institute_of_Mathematical_Statistics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Publications")]

Main category: [Institute of Mathematical Statistics academic journals](/wiki/Category:Institute_of_Mathematical_Statistics_academic_journals "Category:Institute of Mathematical Statistics academic journals")

The Institute publishes five journals:

* *[Annals of Statistics](/wiki/Annals_of_Statistics "Annals of Statistics")*
* *[Annals of Applied Statistics](/wiki/Annals_of_Applied_Statistics "Annals of Applied Statistics")*
* *[Annals of Probability](/wiki/Annals_of_Probability "Annals of Probability")*
* *[Annals of Applied Probability](/wiki/Annals_of_Applied_Probability "Annals of Applied Probability")*
* *[Statistical Science](/wiki/Statistical_Science "Statistical Science")*

In addition, it co-sponsors:

* *[Electronic Communications in Probability](/wiki/Electronic_Communications_in_Probability "Electronic Communications in Probability")*
* *[Electronic Journal of Probability](/wiki/Electronic_Journal_of_Probability "Electronic Journal of Probability")*
* *[Electronic Journal of Statistics](/wiki/Electronic_Journal_of_Statistics "Electronic Journal of Statistics")*
* *[Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics](/wiki/Journal_of_Computational_and_Graphical_Statistics "Journal of Computational and Graphical Statistics")* (A joint publication with the [American Statistical Association](/wiki/American_Statistical_Association "American Statistical Association") and the [Interface Foundation of North America](/w/index.php?title=Interface_Foundation_of_North_America&action=edit&redlink=1 "Interface Foundation of North America (page does not exist)"))
* *[Probability Surveys](/wiki/Probability_Surveys "Probability Surveys")* (A joint publication with the [International Statistical Institute](/wiki/International_Statistical_Institute "International Statistical Institute") and the Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability)
* *[Statistics Surveys](/wiki/Statistics_Surveys "Statistics Surveys")* (A joint publication with the American Statistical Association, the Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability, and the [Statistical Society of Canada](/wiki/Statistical_Society_of_Canada "Statistical Society of Canada"))

There are also some *affiliated* journals:

* *[Probability and Mathematical Statistics](/wiki/Probability_and_Mathematical_Statistics "Probability and Mathematical Statistics")* (Wrocław University of Technology)
* *[Latin American Journal of Probability and Mathematical Statistics](/w/index.php?title=Latin_American_Journal_of_Probability_and_Mathematical_Statistics&action=edit&redlink=1 "Latin American Journal of Probability and Mathematical Statistics (page does not exist)")*

Furthermore, five journals are *supported* by the IMS:

* *[Annales de l'Institut Henri Poincaré](/wiki/Annales_de_l%27Institut_Henri_Poincar%C3%A9 "Annales de l'Institut Henri Poincaré")*
* *[Bayesian Analysis](/wiki/Bayesian_Analysis_(journal) "Bayesian Analysis (journal)")* (published by the International Society for Bayesian Analysis)
* *Bernoulli* (published by the [Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability](/wiki/Bernoulli_Society_for_Mathematical_Statistics_and_Probability "Bernoulli Society for Mathematical Statistics and Probability"))
* *[Brazilian Journal of Probability and Statistics](/wiki/Brazilian_Journal_of_Probability_and_Statistics "Brazilian Journal of Probability and Statistics")* (published by the [Brazilian Statistical Association](/wiki/Brazilian_Statistical_Association "Brazilian Statistical Association"))
* *[Stochastic Systems](/w/index.php?title=Stochastic_Systems&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stochastic Systems (page does not exist)")*

## Fellows

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Institute_of_Mathematical_Statistics&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Fellows")]

See also: [Category: Fellows of the Institute of Mathematical Statistics](/wiki/Category:Fellows_of_the_Institute_of_Mathematical_Statistics "Category:Fellows of the Institute of Mathematical Statistics")

The IMS selects an annual class of Fellows who have demonstrated distinction in research or leadership in statistics or probability.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Meetings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Institute_of_Mathematical_Statistics&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Meetings")]

Meetings gives scholars and practitioners a platform to present research results, disseminate job opportunities and exchange ideas with each other. The IMS holds an annual meeting called [Joint Statistical Meetings](/wiki/Joint_Statistical_Meetings "Joint Statistical Meetings") (JSM),[[2]](#cite_note-2) and sponsors multiple international meetings, for example, [Spring Research Conference](/wiki/Spring_Research_Conference "Spring Research Conference") (SRC).[[3]](#cite_note-3)
